quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 5, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Virology: Structure, Replication, and Viral Diseases
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Virus Classification, Genetic Material, Capsid, Envelope, Lytic Cycle, Retroviruses, Reverse Transcriptase, Viral Diseases, Virus Size, Prions, Plant Virus Transmission

questions.question: Viruses are classified as:
questions.explanation: Viruses are acellular infectious agents that consist of genetic material surrounded by a protein coat. They are not cells and are not classified as prokaryotes (bacteria) or eukaryotes (fungi, plants, animals).

questions.question: The genetic material in viruses can be:
questions.explanation: Viruses can have either DNA or RNA as their genetic material, but never both. DNA viruses include adenoviruses and herpesviruses. RNA viruses include influenza virus, HIV, and rhinoviruses.

questions.question: Which of the following structures is found in all viruses?
questions.explanation: All viruses have a capsid (protein coat) that encloses their genetic material. Envelopes are found only in some viruses, tail fibers only in bacteriophages, and flagella are not viral structures.

questions.question: Some viruses have an outer lipid membrane derived from the host called:
questions.explanation: The viral envelope is a lipid membrane derived from the host cell's membrane (nuclear or plasma membrane) during viral budding. It contains viral proteins and helps in host cell entry.

questions.question: Viruses cannot reproduce independently because they:
questions.explanation: Viruses lack ribosomes and other cellular machinery needed for protein synthesis and energy production. They must hijack host cell ribosomes to produce viral proteins and replicate.

questions.question: The process by which a virus injects its genetic material and produces new viruses inside a host cell is called:
questions.explanation: Viral infection is the process where a virus enters a host cell, replicates its genetic material, and produces new viral particles. Binary fission is bacterial reproduction, transcription is RNA synthesis, and replication is DNA duplication.

questions.question: In the lytic cycle, viruses:
options: Integrate into the host genome without killing the host
Cause immediate host cell lysis and release new viruses
questions.explanation: In the lytic cycle, viruses replicate rapidly and cause the host cell to burst (lyse), releasing new virions. The lysogenic cycle involves viral DNA integration into the host genome without immediate cell death.

questions.question: Select all that apply: Viruses that cause the common cold are typically:
questions.explanation: Common colds are primarily caused by rhinoviruses (most common) and adenoviruses. Retroviruses include HIV, bacteriophages infect bacteria, and prions are infectious proteins.

questions.question: HIV is a type of:
questions.explanation: HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) is a retrovirus, meaning it carries RNA and uses reverse transcriptase to make DNA from its RNA genome. This DNA then integrates into the host genome.

questions.question: Which enzyme allows retroviruses to synthesize DNA from RNA?
questions.explanation: Reverse transcriptase is an enzyme unique to retroviruses that converts RNA into DNA. This is the reverse of normal transcription (DNA to RNA). DNA polymerase synthesizes DNA, RNA polymerase synthesizes RNA, and ligase joins DNA fragments.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by a virus?
questions.explanation: Influenza is caused by the influenza virus. Tuberculosis is bacterial, malaria is protozoan, and ringworm is fungal.

questions.question: Viruses are generally smaller than bacteria, typically ranging from:
questions.explanation: Most viruses range from 20 to 300 nanometers in diameter. Bacteria are typically 1-10 micrometers (1000-10,000 nm), making viruses much smaller and visible only with electron microscopes.

questions.question: Viral envelopes are usually acquired from:
questions.explanation: Viral envelopes are derived from host cell membranes (plasma membrane, nuclear membrane, or endoplasmic reticulum) when viruses bud from the cell. The virus modifies the membrane with its own glycoproteins.

questions.question: Prions differ from viruses because they:
questions.explanation: Prions are infectious proteins with no genetic material (no DNA or RNA). They cause disease by inducing misfolding of normal cellular proteins. Viruses always contain either DNA or RNA.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT true about viruses?
options: They have either DNA or RNA
They can reproduce outside a host cell
questions.explanation: Viruses cannot reproduce outside a host cell—they are obligate intracellular parasites. They require host cell machinery for replication. All other statements are true.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered virus structure and replication cycles! 🦠
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between lytic and lysogenic cycles.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on viral genetic material and retrovirus enzymes.
scoreMessages.0: Please review virus classification, capsid function, and viral diseases.
